triage_flags.notes: mana_cost: {2}{G} // {3}{G} present in frontmatter; green-magic present in tags_filter.

trivia_bullets.text: Greenhouse // Rickety Gazebo is one of DSK's Room cards: split enchantments where each half is a 'door' that unlocks separately. The Room mechanic was new to Duskmourn (released 2024-09-27) and represents the haunted house's rooms as persistent enchantments with dual states.
sources: magic.wizards.com: Duskmourn: House of Horror Mechanics

trivia_bullets.text: Greenhouse (cost {2}{G}) upgrades all lands to produce any color of mana — strong mana-fixing. Rickety Gazebo (cost {3}{G}) mills four cards and returns up to two permanent cards among them to hand — enabling graveyard recursion. The two halves combine mana fixing with card selection.

EDHREC rank 6,637 at time of Scryfall pull, highest of the five cards in this batch. Approximately 19,584 tracked Commander decks include it per EDHREC. Marina Vendrell (DSK's Room-themed commander) runs it in approximately 94% of her decks tracked on EDHREC.
sources: edhrec.com: Greenhouse // Rickety Gazebo card page via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: Previewed on the Limited Resources Patreon (2024-09-06), indicating Wizards selected it as a card of limited-format interest. Illustrated by John Di Giovanni, who illustrated both halves of the card; the split-panel art is a single commission divided across the Room card layout.
John Di Giovanni has approximately 33-57 card credits in MTG (range reflects variant printings). No Hipsters of the Coast artist profile found.
sources: artofmtg.com: John Di Giovanni artist page via WebSearch
gatherer.wizards.com: artist search John Di Giovanni
